name: ZeroStack
description: Minimal, resource-efficient CLI AI coding agent written in Rust with multi-provider LLM support and configurable permission modes.

developer.comment: Solo open-source developer; community support via Matrix chat.

first-release.comment: Repo created 2026-05-12; first tagged release v1.0.1 on 2026-05-16. Rapid versioning (v1.4.5 by 2026-06-04). Checked 2026-06-05.

status.comment: Actively developed; releases every few days as of June 2026.

specialization.comment: Broad coding agent; differentiator is minimal resource footprint, not a niche domain.

interface.comment: Terminal CLI with a TUI (markdown rendering). Can also act as an ACP server for editor integration, but ships as a CLI tool.

host-ides.comment: Runs in the terminal. Optional ACP feature lets it serve editors like Zed via JSON-RPC, but it is not itself an IDE.

open-source.comment: GPL-3.0-only; full source on GitHub and crates.io.

default-model-provider.comment: OpenRouter is the default endpoint, but it is a router across many providers; also supports OpenAI-compatible, Anthropic, Gemini, Ollama, and custom endpoints. No single first-party model.

model-choice.comment: Switch models at runtime via the /model command; multiple providers configurable.

bring-your-own-key.comment: BYOK only — user supplies API keys via per-provider environment variables; there is no vendor billing.

local-model-support.comment: Ollama integration plus any OpenAI-compatible local endpoint (custom base URL).

multi-file-editing.comment: Full file-access tools (read/write/edit) plus grep/find across the codebase.

autonomous-task-execution.comment: Iterative 'Ralph Wiggum loops' chain edit/run/fix steps toward a goal; git-worktree branch-per-task workflow.

terminal-command-execution.comment: Bash execution tool with permission gating and optional Bubblewrap sandbox.

mcp-support.comment: Documented MCP support to connect external tool servers; exposed as an optional compile-time feature, configured in the config file.

subagents-parallel-agents.comment: Subagents for parallel codebase exploration; optional experimental 'multithread' feature for multi-threaded subagents.

codebase-indexing.comment: File-based retrieval (grep, find_files, read) with no semantic/embeddings index.

planning-mode.comment: Explicit 'Plan' prompt: planning-only mode that explores and produces a plan without writing code.

multimodal-input.comment: No image input documented as of June 2026.

browser-computer-use.comment: Has WebFetch/WebSearch tools (via Exa) but cannot drive a browser/GUI to act and verify.

operating-mode.comment: Interactive TUI chat; permission modes range from per-action approval (guarded/restrictive) to fully autonomous (yolo/standard loops). No inline completion or detached async runner.

inline-completion.comment: Terminal CLI agent; no as-you-type tab completion.

background-async-execution.comment: Runs in a foreground terminal session; no detached/cloud async dispatch. Sessions can be saved and resumed but the agent does not run while the user walks away.

persistent-memory.comment: Optional 'memory' build feature: plain-markdown global MEMORY.md plus per-project daily logs injected into the system prompt each session. Gated (cargo install --features memory).

checkpoints-rollback.comment: Session save/load/resume preserves conversation state, and git-worktrees isolate tasks per branch, but there is no built-in working-tree checkpoint/restore that reverts an agent run as a unit (relies on git).

git-integration.comment: Native git-worktree integration (/worktree): branch-per-task, automatic merging and cleanup.

pr-creation.comment: No pull-request creation documented; git integration is worktree/branch-level.

custom-rules-instructions.comment: Auto-loads AGENTS.md or CLAUDE.md from project root or any ancestor; optional ARCHITECTURE.md (archmd feature) injected into the system prompt.

hooks-extensibility.comment: Extensible via MCP servers, custom providers, and custom prompts placed in $XDG_CONFIG_HOME/zerostack/prompts/. No documented event-hook system.

permission-model.comment: Five configurable modes (restrictive, readonly, guarded, standard, yolo) with per-tool glob patterns; optional Bubblewrap sandbox for bash.

self-host-on-prem.comment: Runs entirely on the user's machine; can run fully offline against a local Ollama model.

privacy-mode.comment: Local-first BYOK tool: code is only sent to the provider the user configures; with Ollama/local endpoints nothing leaves the machine. No vendor collects or trains on code (open-source, no vendor billing).

enterprise-controls.comment: Single-developer OSS CLI; no SSO/RBAC/audit-log/VPC enterprise governance features.

pricing-model.comment: The agent is free and open-source; users pay only their own model-provider costs (BYOK), or nothing when running local models.

free-tier.comment: Entire tool is free (GPL-3.0). Only third-party model API usage may cost money.

entry-price-usd-mo.comment: Free open-source software; no subscription. BYOK provider costs are separate.

maturity.comment: Early but fast-moving (first release 2026-05; ~1.1k stars by 2026-06). Single maintainer, frequent breaking-ish releases, several features gated/experimental. Usable but evolving.

community-size.comment: ~1,105 GitHub stars as of 2026-06-05; Hacker News and Medium coverage. New single-vendor project.

extension-plugin-ecosystem.comment: Consumes the MCP ecosystem but has no marketplace or meaningful third-party plugin ecosystem of its own.

project-memory-files.comment: Auto-loads AGENTS.md or CLAUDE.md from project root or any ancestor; ARCHITECTURE.md (custom, archmd feature) also injected for design context.

memory-scopes.comment: Global/user memory in $XDG_DATA_HOME plus per-project daily logs; AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md loaded from root or any ancestor directory (folder-level). No enterprise/org policy scope.

scoped-rules-files.comment: AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md/ARCHITECTURE.md are always-on context files; no path-glob or file-type conditional rule activation documented.

auto-memory.comment: README states the agent does not automatically generate memory entries; the gated memory feature provides persistent markdown notes but they are not auto-written by the agent.

skill-support.comment: Ten built-in mode prompts (code, plan, review, debug, ask, brainstorm, frontend-design, review-security, simplify, write-prompt) plus user-defined custom prompts loaded on demand.

skill-invocation.comment: Prompts are selected by the user via the /prompt slash command; no documented autonomous model-side skill selection.

always-on-surface.comment: Prompts are switchable modes — one active prompt is loaded at a time; only their names are surfaced for selection, not all bodies. Interpreted per Always-On Surface guideline; checked 2026-06-05.

skill-distribution.comment: Custom prompts are created as files in $XDG_CONFIG_HOME/zerostack/prompts/; no marketplace or registry.
